Title: Allelic variation of BBX24 is a dominant determinant controlling red coloration and dwarfism in pear

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 2-oxidation that deactivates bioactive gibberellins, reducing active GA pool
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product pool by depleting bioactive GA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for GA deactivation not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stem elongation and plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Decreases bioactive GA content and causes dwarfism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in stems of red-dwarf pear plants but not green-standard plants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression in transgenic PyBBX24ΔN14 tobacco than in PyBBX24 or EV controls
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous GA3 treatment rescues dwarf phenotype; greenhouse/MS medium growth
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive) for overexpression in tobacco; native PyGA2ox8 promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; stable overexpression in tobacco; endogenous GA quantification; GA3 rescue assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PyGA2ox8 is expressed in red-dwarf pear stems but not in green-standard stems. 2. PyBBX24ΔN14 directly activates the PyGA2ox8 promoter by binding a G-box motif. 3. PyGA2ox8 overexpression in tobacco causes dwarfism similar to PyBBX24ΔN14 lines, linking GA deactivation to reduced height.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced plant height/dwarfism due to depletion of active GA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering compact tree architecture, but likely should be balanced against excessive dwarfing; can be coupled with pigmentation traits through upstream BBX24 alleles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats PyGA2ox8 as a direct downstream effector of PyBBX24ΔN14-mediated dwarfism; PyBBX24 also binds the promoter but does not activate dwarfing to the same extent.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Pyrus pyrifolia; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Solanum lycopersicum; Fragaria vesca
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Red Zaosu × Cuiyu F1; Col-0 and bbx24 mutant; Samsun; Micro-Tom; Yellow Wonder 5AF7
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B-box (BBX) zinc finger transcription factor with two B-box domains, C-terminal NLS and VP motif
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins provide red coloration and stress-associated pigmentation; bioactive GAs promote stem elongation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Young stems; leaves; fruit peel; flowers; roots (for anthocyanin); stems (for GA effect)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type PyBBX24 does not promote anthocyanin accumulation and promotes normal growth rather than dwarfism
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and GA catabolism-related transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Context-dependent transcriptional regulation; interacts with HY5 but does not activate key anthocyanin promoters in tested assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: No significant activation of PyUFGT or PyMYB10 promoters in reporter assays; binds PyGA2ox8 promoter but does not confer strong dwarfing phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Represents the non-mutant allele whose C-terminal NLS/VP motifs repress the gain-of-function seen in mutant alleles
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light-related BBX/HY5 signaling context inferred; high-light treatment used in pear calli assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in transgenic systems; endogenous allele compared between red and green progeny
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Continuous high light in pear calli; transient assays in N. benthamiana; GA3 rescue context for growth assays
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive); pSAK277 expression cassette in transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL/BSA/SV mapping; overexpression; complementation in Arabidopsis bbx24 mutant; dual-luciferase assay; Y2H; BiFC; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PyBBX24 is the wild-type BBX24 allele located in the mapped LG4/Chr4 interval. 2. Unlike the mutant alleles, PyBBX24 does not induce anthocyanin accumulation in pear, strawberry, tobacco, or tomato assays. 3. PyBBX24 complements the Arabidopsis bbx24 mutant short-hypocotyl phenotype, supporting a growth-promoting role. 4. Its C-terminal NLS and VP motifs repress the gain-of-function activities seen in truncated alleles.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Wild-type allele is less useful for red coloration/dwarfing breeding but defines the functional baseline; C-terminal motif editing could convert it into useful gain-of-function alleles
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although PyBBX24 can interact with PyHY5 and bind the PyGA2ox8 promoter, it lacks the strong activation phenotype of mutant alleles. The paper emphasizes C-terminal NLS and VP motifs as key repressive determinants.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; whole-genome resequencing; BSA-seq; SRA: PRJNA853469

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Pyrus pyrifolia; Fragaria vesca; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Solanum lycopersicum
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Red Zaosu × Cuiyu F1; Yellow Wonder 5AF7; Col-0; Samsun; Micro-Tom
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Truncated B-box zinc finger transcription factor; two N-terminal B-box domains retained, C-terminal NLS and VP motifs lost due to 14-bp deletion
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins confer red coloration; bioactive GAs promote stem elongation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Young stems; leaves; fruit peel; pear calli; tobacco flowers; tomato leaves/flowers/roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and reduces plant height by decreasing bioactive GA levels
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis activation and GA deactivation pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; direct promoter binding for PyGA2ox8; indirect activation of PyUFGT and PyMYB10 via interaction with PyHY5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PyUFGT and PyMYB10 promoters in a HY5-dependent manner; directly activates PyGA2ox8 promoter via G-box binding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major dominant determinant controlling red coloration and dwarfism in pear; gain-of-function mutant allele
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Allelic truncation caused by 14-bp deletion; light/HY5-related signaling context; GA3 treatment reveals downstream GA deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mutant allele identified in red-dwarf progeny and red sport germplasm
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive transgene expression in pear calli, Arabidopsis, tobacco, tomato, and transient fruit assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Continuous high light in calli enhances pigmentation assay; NtHY5 RNAi suppresses activity; exogenous GA3 rescues dwarfism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA-seq; QTL mapping; structural variation analysis; overexpression in multiple species; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of PyBBX24; dual-luciferase assay; Y2H; BiFC; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; metabolite profiling; GA quantification; GA3 rescue
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A 14-bp deletion in exon 3 creates a frameshifted PyBBX24ΔN14 allele lacking the C-terminal NLS and VP motifs. 2. PyBBX24ΔN14 overexpression induces anthocyanin accumulation in pear calli, pear fruit, strawberry fruit, Arabidopsis, tobacco, and tomato, while wild-type PyBBX24 does not. 3. PyBBX24ΔN14 activates PyUFGT and PyMYB10 through interaction with PyHY5, which directly binds their promoters. 4. PyBBX24ΔN14 directly binds and activates the PyGA2ox8 promoter, reduces bioactive GA levels, and causes dwarfism. 5. GA3 application rescues the dwarf phenotype, confirming GA deficiency as the mechanism.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong dwarfism and compact growth may be beneficial for high-density planting but could impose excessive height reduction depending on deployment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value dominant allele for simultaneous improvement of red peel/foliage coloration and compact architecture in pear and possibly other crops; promising for marker-assisted breeding or precise genome editing to create C-terminal truncations, but requires balancing degree of dwarfism
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The NLS and VP motifs in the wild-type C-terminus suppress this gain-of-function. Similar independent premature-stop alleles were found in unrelated red pear sports, supporting repeated selection.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; whole-genome resequencing; BSA-seq; SRA: PRJNA853469

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Pyrus pyrifolia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Nicotiana tabacum
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates anthocyanin accumulation by enabling/strengthening PyBBX24ΔN14 activation of anthocyanin genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct promoter binding and co-activation with BBX24ΔN14
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly binds G-box elements in PyUFGT and PyMYB10 promoters; enhances PyBBX24ΔN14-mediated promoter activation, especially for PyUFGT
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally essential co-regulator for full anthocyanin-promoting activity of PyBBX24ΔN14
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light-related signaling context inferred for HY5
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms heterodimer with PyBBX24ΔN14 and PyBBX24
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NtHY5 RNAi reduces PyBBX24ΔN14-mediated pigmentation in transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for overexpression constructs; NtHY5 RNAi used in tobacco assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; Y2H; BiFC; dual-luciferase assay; RNAi suppression assays; transient pigmentation assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PyHY5 directly binds G-box elements in the promoters of PyUFGT and PyMYB10 in vitro and in vivo. 2. PyHY5 interacts with both PyBBX24 and PyBBX24ΔN14. 3. Silencing NtHY5 sharply reduces the anthocyanin-promoting effect of PyBBX24ΔN14, indicating HY5 is required for full activity. 4. PyHY5 enhances PyBBX24ΔN14-mediated activation of PyUFGT and supports anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking partner or pathway context requirement when deploying BBX24 truncation alleles for pigmentation engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although important mechanistically, PyHY5 is presented primarily as the cofactor through which PyBBX24ΔN14 acts on anthocyanin genes rather than the mapped causal allele.
